_comment: Golden canonicalization vectors for design doc §7.2 / issue #11. Each entry's 'fields' is the JSON object passed to netbbs.boards.content_id.canonical_json_bytes/compute_content_id. 'canonical_hex' is the exact resulting canonical UTF-8 bytes, hex-encoded. 'content_id' is BLAKE2b-256 (hex) of those exact bytes. An independent, non-Python implementation of the canonical format is compatible if and only if it reproduces canonical_hex byte-for-byte (and therefore content_id) for every vector here. See tests/test_link_canonical_vectors.py, which asserts the current Python implementation against this same file.

vectors.name: sorted_keys_regardless_of_construction_order
vectors.description: Object keys are sorted lexicographically in the canonical bytes, independent of the order fields were constructed in.

vectors.name: same_logical_content_different_field_order
vectors.description: Identical logical content built with fields in a different order produces byte-identical canonical output and the same content_id as the previous vector.

vectors.name: nfc_and_nfd_forms_normalize_identically
vectors.description: The NFD-decomposed input string ('cafe' + combining acute accent, U+0301) must be recursively NFC-normalized before serialization; the canonical bytes below contain the single precomposed U+00E9 codepoint ('\u00e9'), not the two-codepoint NFD sequence.

vectors.name: omitted_optional_field_differs_from_explicit_null
vectors.description: An explicit JSON null is a distinct canonical value from the field being absent entirely -- this vector is the explicit-null case ({"parent_post_id": null}), which must NOT collide with the content_id of an empty object.

vectors.name: nested_dict_and_list_structure
vectors.description: Normalization and key-sorting recurse into nested dicts/lists; a bool is serialized as true/false, never mistaken for the integer 1/0.

vectors.name: full_envelope_with_key_transition_shaped_payload
vectors.description: A complete round-27 envelope ({netbbs_protocol, object_type, payload}) shaped like a real key_transition event -- object_type participates in the hashed/signed bytes, which is what makes it mandatory domain separation rather than caller convention.

vectors.name: integer_at_safe_boundary
vectors.description: 2^53 - 1, the largest integer exactly representable as an IEEE-754 double (JSON.parse's/JavaScript's safe integer bound), is the largest permitted integer value -- see the corresponding *_beyond_safe_boundary rejection tests in tests/test_content_id.py for the value one past this.
